steps.title: Fastest path: mark the givens first
steps.content: Track the given angle relationships first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Apply triangle angle sum property
steps.content:
The sum of all angles in a triangle equals $180^{\circ}$. With ∠J = $42^{\circ}$ and ∠K = $42^{\circ}$:
$$\angle J + \angle K + \angle L = 180^{\circ}$$
$$42^{\circ} + 42^{\circ} + \angle L = 180^{\circ}$$
$$84^{\circ} + \angle L = 180^{\circ}$$
$$\angle L = 96^{\circ}$$
B is correct.
